 coefficient of the order-$r$ vertex in the universal bulk
 effective action determined by~$\cA$
 \textup{(}Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:shadow-tower-holographic-interpretation}}\textup{)}.
\end{enumerate}
The three descriptions produce the \emph{same} element of
$\cA^{\mathrm{sh}}_{r,0}$. In particular, the $L_\infty$-formality
obstruction tower of the minimal model, the recursive shadow
obstruction tower of the modular cyclic deformation complex, and
the holographic correction hierarchy for boundary-bulk
reconstruction coincide as graded objects.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
The identification $(i) \Leftrightarrow (ii)$ is
Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}, whose proof
proceeds by the four-step induction recorded in that theorem.
For $(i) \Leftrightarrow (iii)$, we invoke the bar-intrinsic
character of~$\Theta_{\cA}$: by
Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}, $\Theta_{\cA} = D_{\cA} - d_0$
is determined by the bar differential of~$\cA$ alone, so every
degree-$r$ component $\Theta_{\cA}^{(r,0)} = S_r(\cA)$ is a
bar-invariant of~$\cA$. On the other hand, the boundary-bulk
reconstructor $\cA \mapsto Z^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is
the derived chiral centre functor
\textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:holo-comp-bulk-reconstruction}}\textup{)},
whose order-$r$ expansion coefficient is, by construction, the
degree-$r$ chiral Hochschild cocycle extracted from the same
bar-intrinsic~$\Theta_{\cA}$. The two extractions use the same
bar differential, so the two coefficient sequences agree degree
by degree. The resulting triple identification is a genus-$0$
statement: the genus-$g$ corrections for $g \geq 1$ belong to the
quantum layer and are discussed separately in
Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:shadow-tower-linfty-formality}}.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Genus-$0$ and all-genus parts of $\operatorname{Sh}_r$]
\label{rem:shadow-genus0-allgenus-decomposition}
\index{shadow obstruction tower!genus-0 vs.\ all-genus parts}
\index{holographic correction hierarchy!genus decomposition}
The three-lens identification of
Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:shadow-tower-three-lenses}} is an
equality at the genus-$0$ level. The degree-$r$ shadow admits the
decomposition
\[
 \operatorname{Sh}_r(\cA)
 \;=\;
 \underbrace{\operatorname{Sh}_r^{(0)}(\cA)}_{\text{genus-$0$,
 classical bulk correction}}
 \;+\;
 \sum_{g \geq 1}
 \underbrace{\operatorname{Sh}_{r,g}(\cA)}_{\text{genus-$g$,
 quantum bulk correction}},
\]
where $\operatorname{Sh}_r^{(0)}$ is the genus-$0$ formality
obstruction identified by
Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}} and
$\operatorname{Sh}_{r,g}$ for $g \geq 1$ is the quantum
$L_\infty$-correction coming from the BV loop operator~$\Lambda_P$
\textup{(}Lemma~\textup{\ref{lem:graph-sum-truncation}}\textup{)}.
Under the holographic lens, the two layers correspond to the
classical and quantum contributions to the bulk effective action
built by the reconstructor
$\cA \mapsto Z^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$: the genus-$0$
part is the tree-level bulk vertex, the genus-$\geq 1$ part is the
loop correction. Conflating the two types miscounts the order of
the holographic correction; the correct accounting keeps them as
separate terms in the double expansion.
\end{remark}

\begin{proposition}[Shadow--formality identification at higher degrees]
\label{prop:shadow-formality-higher-degree}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{shadow obstruction tower!formality identification!degrees 5--7}
\index{Linfty@$L_\infty$-formality!shadow tower identification!higher degrees}
\index{Ainfty@$A_\infty$-operations!$m_5$, $m_6$, $m_7$}
The shadow--formality identification of
Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:shadow-formality-low-degree}} and
Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}} holds
constructively at degrees $5$, $6$, $7$:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item The transferred $A_\infty$-operation $m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}$ on the
 primary line of $H^*(B(\mathrm{Vir}_c))$ satisfies
 \begin{equation}\label{eq:mk-primary-line-shadow}
 m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}(s^{-1}T, \dotsc, s^{-1}T)
 \;=\; S_k \cdot e_{2k},
 \qquad k = 5, 6, 7,
 \end{equation}
 where $s^{-1}T$ is the desuspension of the conformal vector $T$
 in bar degree~$1$, $e_{2k}$ is the weight-$2k$ basis
 vector of $\operatorname{Ext}^1$, and $S_k$ is the
 degree-$k$ shadow coefficient:
 \begin{alignat}{2}
 S_5 &= -\frac{48}{c^2(5c{+}22)}, &\qquad&
 \textup{(quintic shadow)}, \label{eq:S5-ainfty} \\
 S_6 &= \frac{80(45c{+}193)}{3\,c^3(5c{+}22)^2},
 &\qquad&
 \textup{(sextic shadow)}, \label{eq:S6-ainfty} \\
 S_7 &= -\frac{2880(15c{+}61)}{7\,c^4(5c{+}22)^2},
 &\qquad&
 \textup{(septic shadow)}. \label{eq:S7-ainfty}
 \end{alignat}
\item The transferred degree-$k$ $L_\infty$ bracket
 $\ell_k^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$ evaluated on the
 truncated MC element satisfies
 $\operatorname{Sh}_k(\cA) = \ell_k^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}
 (\Theta^{\leq k-1}, \dotsc, \Theta^{\leq k-1})$
 at degrees $k = 5, 6, 7$, consistently with the
 all-degree identification
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}}).
\item The Stasheff $A_\infty$ relations at degrees $5$, $6$, $7$
 are satisfied: on the primary line they reduce to
 the Maurer--Cartan equation of the shadow obstruction tower, which is
 equivalent to the convolution identity $f(t)^2 = Q_L(t)$
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:riccati-algebraicity}}).
\item The depth classification is confirmed at these degrees:
 \begin{center}
 \small
 \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.1}
 \begin{tabular}{lccccl}
 \toprule
 \textbf{Family} &
 $m_5^{\mathrm{tr}}$ & $m_6^{\mathrm{tr}}$ &
 $m_7^{\mathrm{tr}}$ & \textbf{Class} &
 \textbf{Mechanism} \\
 \midrule
 Heisenberg & $0$ & $0$ & $0$ & $\mathbf{G}$ &
 coassociative bar \\
 Affine KM & $0$ & $0$ & $0$ & $\mathbf{L}$ &
 Jacobi kills degree~$\geq 4$ \\
 $\beta\gamma$ & $0$ & $0$ & $0$ & $\mathbf{C}$ &
 stratum separation at~$5$ \\
 $\mathrm{Vir}_c$ & $\neq 0$ & $\neq 0$ & $\neq 0$ &
 $\mathbf{M}$ &
 MC forces all $S_k \neq 0$ \\
 \bottomrule
 \end{tabular}
 \end{center}
\end{enumerate}
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
\emph{Part~(i): derivation of $m_5^{\mathrm{tr}}$, $m_6^{\mathrm{tr}}$,
$m_7^{\mathrm{tr}}$ on the primary line.}

The Kadeishvili--Merkulov tree formula
(equation~\eqref{eq:tree-formula-general}) computes the
transferred $A_\infty$ operations as sums over planar rooted
trees. On the primary line, all inputs have weight~$2$ and bar
degree~$1$, so the composition $m_j(\dotsc, m_i(\dotsc), \dotsc)$
maps to a \emph{single} weight component in $\operatorname{Ext}^1$.
The tree formula then reduces to the scalar convolution recursion
from the shadow metric identity $f(t)^2 = Q_L(t)$.

Write $f(t) = \sum_{n \geq 0} a_n\, t^n$ with
$a_0 = c$, $a_1 = 6$, $a_2 = 40/(c(5c{+}22))$, and the
recursion
\begin{equation}\label{eq:convolution-higher-recursion}
 a_n
 \;=\;
 -\frac{1}{2c}\sum_{j=1}^{n-1} a_j\, a_{n-j},
 \qquad n \geq 3,
\end{equation}
which encodes the vanishing of the $t^n$-coefficient ($n \geq 3$)
in the polynomial $Q_L(t) - f(t)^2$. The shadow obstruction tower
coefficient is $S_r = a_{r-2}/r$.

\emph{At degree~$5$}:
$a_3 = -a_1 a_2 / c = -6 \cdot 40/(c^2(5c{+}22)) = -240/(c^2(5c{+}22))$,
hence
$S_5 = a_3/5 = -48/(c^2(5c{+}22))$.

\emph{At degree~$6$}:
$a_4 = -(2a_1 a_3 + a_2^2)/(2c)$. Substituting:
\begin{align*}
 2a_1 a_3 &= 2 \cdot 6 \cdot \frac{-240}{c^2(5c{+}22)}
 = \frac{-2880}{c^2(5c{+}22)}, \\[3pt]
 a_2^2 &= \frac{1600}{c^2(5c{+}22)^2}, \\[3pt]
 a_4 &= -\frac{-2880/(c^2(5c{+}22)) + 1600/(c^2(5c{+}22)^2)}{2c} \\
 &= \frac{2880(5c{+}22) - 1600}{2c^3(5c{+}22)^2}
 = \frac{14400c + 61760}{2c^3(5c{+}22)^2}
 = \frac{160(45c{+}193)}{c^3(5c{+}22)^2}.
\end{align*}
Hence $S_6 = a_4/6 = 80(45c{+}193)/(3\,c^3(5c{+}22)^2)$.

\emph{At degree~$7$}: $a_5 = -(a_1 a_4 + a_2 a_3)/c$.
\begin{align*}
 a_1 a_4 &= \frac{960(45c{+}193)}{c^3(5c{+}22)^2}, &
 a_2 a_3 &= \frac{-9600}{c^3(5c{+}22)^2}, \\
 a_5 &= \frac{-(960(45c{+}193) - 9600)}{c^4(5c{+}22)^2}
 = \frac{-2880(15c{+}61)}{c^4(5c{+}22)^2}.
\end{align*}
The factorization $43200c + 175680 = 2880(15c+61)$ is verified
directly: $2880 \cdot 15 = 43200$ and $2880 \cdot 61 = 175680$.
Hence $S_7 = a_5/7 = -2880(15c+61)/(7\,c^4(5c{+}22)^2)$.

The identification
$m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}(s^{-1}T, \dotsc, s^{-1}T) = S_k \cdot e_{2k}$
then follows from the general shadow--formality identification
(Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}),
which shows that the tree formula at degree~$k$ with all
primary-line inputs reproduces the shadow coefficient~$S_k$.

\medskip
\emph{Part~(ii): $L_\infty$ brackets at degrees $5$--$7$.}

The genus-$0$ bracket $\ell_k^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$ on the minimal
$L_\infty$-model of $\Definfmod(\cA)$ is computed by three
independent routes: the operadic Feynman transform (using
$C_k$ binary trees on $k{+}1$ leaves, where $C_k$ is
the $k$-th Catalan number), the HPL transfer (using
$C_{k-1}$ planar trees on $k$ leaves), and the stable-graph
expansion (using $(2k{-}3)!!$ trivalent trees on $k{+}1$ legs).
All three produce the same bracket value by the universal
property of the Feynman transform.

\emph{At the scalar level} (genus~$0$, rank-$1$ primary line),
$\ell_k^{(0)}$ vanishes for $k \geq 3$: the scalar MC
recursion closes with the binary bracket $\ell_2^{(0)}$ alone.
This is immediate from the shadow metric factorization
$f^2 = Q_L$: the $t^n$-coefficients for $n \geq 3$ all vanish,
and these are precisely the higher-bracket contributions at
the scalar level.

\emph{At the vector level}, the brackets are nontrivial for
class~$\mathbf{M}$ algebras. The evaluation
$\operatorname{Sh}_k(\cA) = \ell_k^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}
(\Theta^{\leq k-1}, \dotsc, \Theta^{\leq k-1})$
holds by the inductive argument of
Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}, Step~3.

For the specific tree counts at degrees $5$ and $6$:
\begin{center}
\small
\begin{tabular}{lcccl}
\toprule
\textbf{Route} & $k = 5$ & $k = 6$ & \textbf{Objects} \\
\midrule
Feynman transform & $C_5 = 42$ & $C_6 = 132$ &
 trees with $k{+}1$ leaves \\
HPL transfer & $C_4 = 14$ & $C_5 = 42$ &
 planar trees with $k$ leaves \\
Stable graph & $(2 \cdot 6 - 5)!! = 105$ & $(2 \cdot 7 - 5)!! = 945$ &
 trivalent trees with $k{+}1$ legs \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
\noindent
The corresponding associahedra are $K_6$ (dimension~$4$,
$f$-vector $(42, 84, 56, 14, 1)$, Euler characteristic~$1$)
and $K_7$ (dimension~$5$,
$f$-vector $(132, 330, 300, 120, 20, 1)$,
Euler characteristic~$1$). The Euler
characteristic equals~$1$ in each case, confirming
contractibility as required.

\medskip
\emph{Part~(iii): $A_\infty$ relations.}

The Stasheff $A_\infty$ relation at degree~$n$ (with $m_1 = 0$
on cohomology) states
\[
 \sum_{\substack{p + q + r = n \\ q \geq 2}}
 (-1)^{pq + r}\,
 m_{p+1+r}(\mathrm{id}^{\otimes p} \otimes m_q \otimes
 \mathrm{id}^{\otimes r})
 \;=\; 0.
\]
On the primary line, this reduces to the MC equation
$2a_0 a_n + \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} a_j a_{n-j} = 0$ for $n \geq 3$,
which is the vanishing of $[t^n](Q_L - f^2)$.
The shadow metric factorization
$f^2 = Q_L$ is established in
Theorem~\ref{thm:riccati-algebraicity}, and it holds
at all orders. At degrees $5$, $6$, $7$, one verifies:
\[
 2c \cdot a_3 + 2a_1 a_2 = 0, \qquad
 2c \cdot a_4 + 2a_1 a_3 + a_2^2 = 0, \qquad
 2c \cdot a_5 + 2a_1 a_4 + 2a_2 a_3 = 0.
\]
Each identity holds by substituting the explicit values
derived above.

\medskip
\emph{Part~(iv): depth verification.}

For classes $\mathbf{G}$, $\mathbf{L}$, and $\mathbf{C}$,
$S_k = 0$ for $k \geq 3$, $k \geq 4$, and $k \geq 5$
respectively
(Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-archetype-classification}),
so $m_k^{\mathrm{tr}} = 0$ on the primary line at these
degrees.

For class~$\mathbf{M}$ (Virasoro): $S_5 \neq 0$
for all $c > 0$ with $5c + 22 \neq 0$
(the denominator $c^2(5c{+}22)$ has no positive zeros).
For $S_6$: the numerator $45c + 193 > 0$ for $c > 0$,
so $S_6 > 0$. For $S_7$: the numerator $15c + 61 > 0$,
so $S_7 < 0$. Hence $m_5^{\mathrm{tr}}$, $m_6^{\mathrm{tr}}$,
$m_7^{\mathrm{tr}}$ are all nonzero for Virasoro at every
$c > 0$, confirming class~$\mathbf{M}$.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[The $L_\infty$ generalized Jacobi identities
at orders $5$ and $6$]
\label{rem:generalized-jacobi-5-6}
\index{Linfty@$L_\infty$-algebra!generalized Jacobi identity}
The generalized Jacobi identity at order~$n$ states
\[
 \sum_{\substack{i + j = n + 1 \\ j \geq 1}}
 \sum_{\sigma \in \operatorname{Sh}(j,n{-}j)}
 \varepsilon(\sigma)\,
 \ell_i\bigl(\ell_j(x_{\sigma(1)}, \dotsc, x_{\sigma(j)}),\,
 x_{\sigma(j+1)}, \dotsc, x_{\sigma(n)}\bigr)
 \;=\; 0.
\]
At the scalar level (genus~$0$) and orders $n = 5, 6$, this
identity is satisfied \emph{trivially}: the inner bracket
$\ell_j$ vanishes for $j \geq 3$ on scalars, $\ell_1$ vanishes
on cocycles, and for $j = 2$ the outer bracket has degree
$n - 1 \geq 4$, which also vanishes on scalars. Thus every
term in the sum is individually zero.

At the \emph{vector level}, the generalized Jacobi identities
at orders $5$ and $6$ impose genuine constraints on the
$L_\infty$ brackets $\ell_5$ and $\ell_6$. These constraints
are satisfied by the shadow obstruction tower data because the tower is
constructed from the MC equation, which is itself the homotopy
Maurer--Cartan equation of the $L_\infty$ structure. The
Jacobi identity at order~$n$ is the \emph{integrability
condition} for the MC equation at that degree.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Formality obstruction cocycles at degrees $5$--$7$]
\label{rem:formality-obstruction-5-7}
\index{formality!obstruction cocycle!higher degree}
\index{shadow depth!infinite tower mechanism}
For the Virasoro algebra at each degree $k \geq 3$, the class
$[m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}] \in HH^{2-k}(\operatorname{Ext},
\operatorname{Ext})$ is a formality obstruction cocycle. The
mechanism forcing $S_k \neq 0$ for all $k \geq 3$ is the
MC recursion~\eqref{eq:convolution-higher-recursion}: the
lower-order shadows $S_2, \dotsc, S_{k-1}$ contribute
through the convolution sum
$\sum_{j=1}^{k-3} a_j a_{k-2-j}$, and this sum is generically
nonzero because the shadow metric $Q_L(t)$ is irreducible over
$\mathbb{Q}(c)$ for Virasoro (class~$\mathbf{M}$).

The explicit forcing at degrees $5$--$7$: at degree~$5$,
the product $a_1 a_2 = 240/(c(5c{+}22)) \neq 0$ forces
$a_3 \neq 0$. At degree~$6$, both $a_1 a_3$ and $a_2^2$
are nonzero; they contribute with the same sign only when
$c$ is at the zero of a specific polynomial (which has no
positive real roots). At degree~$7$, the pair $(a_1 a_4, a_2 a_3)$
forces $a_5 \neq 0$. This inductive mechanism is the
MC equation instantiated at each degree, and it never terminates
for class~$\mathbf{M}$: the critical discriminant
$\Delta = 8\kappa S_4 = 40/(5c{+}22) > 0$ for $c > 0$
implies irreducibility of $Q_L$, which in turn implies
$a_n \neq 0$ for all $n \geq 1$.

No gauge transformation can simultaneously kill all
$[m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}]$ because the shadow obstruction tower is infinite:
any gauge equivalence that kills $m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}$ at degree~$k$
shifts the obstruction to degree $k{+}1$. The intrinsic
non-formality of the Virasoro modular convolution algebra
is thus an infinite-codimension phenomenon, with each degree
adding one independent obstruction class.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Derivative tower and Eulerian weight mechanism]
\label{rem:derivative-tower-eulerian-mechanism}
\index{derivative tower!Eulerian weight mechanism}
\index{Eulerian idempotent!derivative tower}
\index{shadow depth!derivative tower mechanism}
The representation-theoretic mechanism underlying the
infinite shadow depth of class~$\mathbf{M}$ algebras is the
\emph{derivative tower}. For a single generator~$\phi$
of conformal weight~$h$, the desuspended degree of
$s^{-1}\phi$ is $d = h - 1$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:three-bar-complexes}).
When~$d$ is odd (e.g., Virasoro: $h = 2$, $d = 1$), the
$S_n$-action on $(s^{-1}\phi)^{\otimes r}$ is the sign
representation, so the coinvariant space
$\mathrm{Sym}^r(s^{-1}\phi) = \Lambda^r(\mathbb{Q}) = 0$
for all $r \geq 2$. The zero-derivative sector is
\emph{extinct}: it contributes nothing to the shadow tower
beyond $\kappa$.

The tower is rescued by derivatives:
$s^{-1}(\partial^k\phi)$ has desuspended degree $h + k - 1$,
so the parity \emph{alternates} with~$k$.
Even-degree descendants ($k$ odd for Virasoro)
contribute symmetric (Eulerian weight $\geq 2$) components,
while odd-degree descendants ($k$ even for Virasoro) contribute
Harrison (weight~$1$) components. Together, the full
derivative tower $\{\partial^k\phi\}_{k \geq 0}$ populates all
Eulerian weights at all degrees.

Whether this population produces a \emph{finite} or
\emph{infinite} shadow tower is controlled by the critical
discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:single-line-dichotomy}):
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item $\Delta = 0$: the derivative-tower contributions
 cancel, $Q_L$ is a perfect square, and the tower
 terminates (classes $\mathbf{G}$ and $\mathbf{L}$).
\item $\Delta \neq 0$: the contributions accumulate
 irrecoverably, $Q_L$ is irreducible, and
 $S_r \neq 0$ for all $r \geq 4$ (class~$\mathbf{M}$).
\end{enumerate}
For Heisenberg ($h = 1$, $d = 0$ even): the base generator
already sits in the symmetric component; the free-field
OPE ensures $S_r = 0$ for $r \geq 3$ without appealing to
the derivative tower. For affine
$\hat{\mathfrak{g}}_k$: the multiple generators (one per root)
provide mixed parities directly, and the Jacobi identity
(not parity extinction) is the operative
termination mechanism.

Computational verification:
\texttt{compute/lib/eulerian\_weight\_mc\_engine.py}
($67$~tests; $5$-path cross-verification of~$\kappa$
weight classification).
\end{remark}

\begin{proposition}[Explicit $L_\infty$ obstruction classes at degrees $5$ and $6$]
\label{prop:linfty-obstruction-5-6}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{Linfty@$L_\infty$-algebra!bracket $\ell_5$}
\index{Linfty@$L_\infty$-algebra!bracket $\ell_6$}
\index{obstruction class!$o_5$, $o_6$}
\index{shadow obstruction tower!$L_\infty$ brackets at degree 5--6}
Let $\cA$ be a modular Koszul chiral algebra with shadow
tower $\{S_r\}_{r \geq 2}$ and shadow metric
$Q_L(t) = (2\kappa + 3\alpha t)^2 + 2\Delta t^2$ on a
primary line~$L$. Let
$\mathfrak{h} = (\cA^{\mathrm{sh}}_{*,0},\,
\{\ell_r^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}\}_{r \geq 2})$ be the
minimal $L_\infty$-model of the genus-$0$ modular
convolution algebra
$\mathfrak{g}_\cA^{\mathrm{mod},(0)}$ from
Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}}.
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item \emph{Quintic bracket and obstruction class.}
 On the primary line, the transferred bracket satisfies
 \begin{equation}\label{eq:ell5-explicit}
 \ell_5^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}
 (\Theta^{\leq 4},\, \Theta^{\leq 4},\,
 \Theta^{\leq 4},\, \Theta^{\leq 4},\,
 \Theta^{\leq 4})
 \;=\; S_5 \cdot e_5,
 \end{equation}
 where $e_5$ is the degree-$5$ basis vector in the
 shadow algebra. The obstruction class is
 \begin{equation}\label{eq:o5-obstruction}
 o_5(\cA)
 \;=\;
 S_5 \cdot e_5
 \;+\;
 \tfrac{1}{2}\ell_2^{(0)}
 (S_3 \cdot e_3,\, S_4 \cdot e_4)
 \;+\;
 (\text{lower-degree cross-terms}),
 \end{equation}
 and the MC equation at degree~$5$ reads
 $o_5(\cA) = 0$ in $\cA^{\mathrm{sh}}$, which
 on the primary line is equivalent to the recursion
 $a_3 = -a_1 a_2/a_0$
 \textup{(}equation~\eqref{eq:convolution-higher-recursion}\textup{)}.
 The homotopy transfer theorem computes the bracket
 $\ell_5^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$ via
 $C_4 = 14$ planar binary trees with $5$ leaves
 \textup{(}HPL route\textup{)}, equivalently via
 $C_5 = 42$ binary trees with $6$ leaves
 \textup{(}Feynman transform route\textup{)}, or via
 $(2 \cdot 6 - 5)!! = 105$ trivalent trees with $6$
 legs \textup{(}stable-graph route\textup{)}.

\item \emph{Sextic bracket and obstruction class.}
 Similarly,
 \begin{equation}\label{eq:ell6-explicit}
 \ell_6^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}
 (\Theta^{\leq 5}, \dotsc, \Theta^{\leq 5})
 \;=\; S_6 \cdot e_6,
 \end{equation}
 where the MC equation at degree~$6$ absorbs $S_6$
 into the obstruction class $o_6(\cA)$. The three
 routes count $C_5 = 42$ planar trees with $6$ leaves,
 $C_6 = 132$ binary trees with $7$ leaves, and
 $(2 \cdot 7 - 5)!! = 945$ trivalent trees with $7$
 legs, respectively.

\item \emph{Vanishing and non-vanishing.}
 The depth-based vanishing is:
 \begin{center}
 \small
 \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.1}
 \begin{tabular}{lccl}
 \toprule
 \textbf{Class} &
 $\ell_5^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$ &
 $\ell_6^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$ &
 \textbf{Mechanism} \\
 \midrule
 $\mathbf{G}$ \textup{(}Heisenberg\textup{)} &
 $0$ & $0$ &
 tower terminates at degree $2$ \\
 $\mathbf{L}$ \textup{(}affine KM\textup{)} &
 $0$ & $0$ &
 Jacobi kills degree $\geq 4$ \\
 $\mathbf{C}$ \textup{(}$\beta\gamma$\textup{)} &
 $0$ & $0$ &
 stratum separation at degree $5$ \\
 $\mathbf{M}$ \textup{(}Virasoro\textup{)} &
 $\neq 0$ & $\neq 0$ &
 $\Delta \neq 0$ forces $a_n \neq 0$ for all $n$ \\
 \bottomrule
 \end{tabular}
 \end{center}
 For Virasoro: the numerical values
 $S_5 = -48/(c^2(5c{+}22))$ and
 $S_6 = 80(45c{+}193)/(3\,c^3(5c{+}22)^2)$
 are both nonzero for all $c > 0$,
 confirming $\ell_5^{(0),\mathrm{tr}} \neq 0$ and
 $\ell_6^{(0),\mathrm{tr}} \neq 0$.

\item \emph{Generalized Jacobi identities.}
 The generalized Jacobi identity
 $\sum_{i+j=n+1} \sum_{\sigma}
 \varepsilon(\sigma)\,
 \ell_i(\ell_j(x_{\sigma(1)}, \dotsc), \dotsc) = 0$
 at orders $n = 5$ and $n = 6$ is satisfied
 \emph{trivially} at the scalar level: the inner
 bracket $\ell_j$ with $j \geq 3$ vanishes on scalars
 \textup{(}genus-$0$ binary sufficiency\textup{)}, and
 for $j = 2$ the outer bracket has degree $n - 1 \geq 4$,
 which also vanishes on scalars. At the vector level,
 the identities hold because the transferred brackets are
 derived from the MC equation of the shadow obstruction tower
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}},
 Step~$3$\textup{)}.
\end{enumerate}
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
\emph{Parts~(i)--(ii).}
Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification} gives the
all-degree identification
$\operatorname{Sh}_r(\cA) = \ell_r^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}
(\Theta^{\leq r-1}, \dotsc, \Theta^{\leq r-1})$.
Applying this at $r = 5$ and $r = 6$ yields the stated
formulas. The tree counts are: for
$\ell_r^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$, the HPL transfer involves
$C_{r-1}$ planar binary trees with $r$ leaves; the
Feynman transform involves $C_r$ binary trees with $r{+}1$
leaves; the stable-graph expansion involves
$(2(r{+}1)-5)!! = (2r{-}3)!!$ trivalent trees with $r{+}1$
legs. For $r = 5$: $C_4 = 14$, $C_5 = 42$,
$7!! = 105$. For $r = 6$: $C_5 = 42$, $C_6 = 132$,
$9!! = 945$. All three routes produce the same bracket
value by the universal property of the Feynman transform.

\emph{Part~(iii).}
Classes $\mathbf{G}$, $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$: the
shadow obstruction tower terminates at degree $2$, $3$, $4$ respectively
(Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-archetype-classification}), so
$S_r = 0$ for $r \geq r_{\max} + 1$, and the
shadow-formality identification gives
$\ell_r^{(0),\mathrm{tr}} = 0$ for
$r \geq r_{\max} + 1$. In particular,
$\ell_5^{(0),\mathrm{tr}} = \ell_6^{(0),\mathrm{tr}} = 0$
for all three classes.

For class $\mathbf{M}$:
$\Delta = 8\kappa S_4 = 40/(5c{+}22) > 0$ for $c > 0$,
so $Q_L$ is irreducible over $\mathbb{Q}(c)$ and the
convolution recursion produces $a_n \neq 0$ for all
$n \geq 1$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:single-line-dichotomy}). Hence
$S_5 \neq 0$ and $S_6 \neq 0$.

\emph{Part~(iv).}
At the scalar level, the scalar MC equation closes with the
binary bracket $\ell_2^{(0)}$ alone: the recursion
$a_n = -(2a_0)^{-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} a_j a_{n-j}$
involves only $\ell_2^{(0)}$-compositions. The higher
brackets $\ell_k^{(0)}$ for $k \geq 3$ contribute zero at
the scalar level
(Construction~\ref{constr:explicit-convolution-linfty}),
so every term in the Jacobi sum at orders $5$ and $6$
vanishes individually. At the vector level, the
generalized Jacobi identity is the integrability condition
for the MC equation, which holds by the bar-intrinsic
construction
(Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}).
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Alternating signs and the branch-point
mechanism]
\label{rem:alternating-signs-mechanism}
\index{shadow tower!sign alternation!mechanism}
\index{shadow metric!branch points}
The shadow coefficients $S_5$, $S_6$, $S_7$ exhibit the
sign pattern $(-, +, -)$: alternating starting at
degree~$5$. This pattern is not accidental but arises from
the analytic structure of the shadow generating function
$f(t) = \sqrt{Q_L(t)}$.

The shadow metric $Q_L(t) = (2\kappa + 3\alpha t)^2
+ 2\Delta t^2$ is a quadratic polynomial in~$t$ with
leading coefficient
$9\alpha^2 + 2\Delta > 0$ (for class~$\mathbf{M}$).
For $\Delta > 0$, its zeros
\[
 t_\pm
 \;=\;
 \frac{-6\kappa\alpha
 \pm 2\kappa\,i\sqrt{2\Delta}}
 {9\alpha^2 + 2\Delta}
\]
are a \emph{complex conjugate pair} (they are real only
when $\Delta \leq 0$, which forces class~$\mathbf{G}$
or~$\mathbf{L}$). The branch points of $\sqrt{Q_L}$
at $t = t_\pm$ control the Taylor coefficients via the
standard formula
\begin{equation}\label{eq:branch-point-asymptotics}
 a_n
 \;\sim\;
 A \cdot |t_0|^{-n} \cdot n^{-3/2}
 \cos(n\,\theta + \varphi),
 \qquad n \to \infty,
\end{equation}
where $t_0 = t_+$ is the branch point, $|t_0|$ is its
modulus, $\theta = \arg(t_0)$, and $A$, $\varphi$ are
real constants depending on $\kappa$, $\alpha$, $\Delta$.
The cosine factor produces \emph{oscillatory}
Taylor coefficients: the sign of $a_n$ is determined by
$\cos(n\theta + \varphi)$, which alternates approximately
when $\theta$ is close to~$\pi$.

For Virasoro at generic positive~$c$, the argument
$\theta = \arg(t_0)$ satisfies
$\pi/2 < \theta < \pi$, so the cosine oscillates with
period approximately~$2$ (near-alternation). The sign
of $S_r = a_{r-2}/r$ inherits this oscillation:
\[
 \operatorname{sgn}(S_r)
 \;\approx\; (-1)^r
 \quad\text{for } 4 \leq r < r_*(c),
\]
where
$r_*(c) \approx \pi/|\pi - \theta|$
is the first degree where the cosine envelope
drifts far enough to break strict alternation
\textup{(}see Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:alternating-signs}}
for the dependence of $r_*$ on~$c$\textup{)}.
In the semiclassical limit $c \to \infty$:
$\theta \to \pi$ and $r_* \to \infty$, giving exact
alternation at all degrees.

The physical content is that the shadow obstruction tower for
class~$\mathbf{M}$ algebras is an \emph{oscillatory
decay}: each successive degree is exponentially damped
by $|t_0|^{-1}$ and oscillates in sign with period
controlled by the argument of the nearest branch point.
The sign alternation starting at degree~$5$ is thus a
direct consequence of the \emph{irreducibility} of the
shadow metric $Q_L$ over $\mathbb{Q}(c)$: irreducibility
forces complex conjugate branch points, and complex
conjugate branch points force oscillatory Taylor
coefficients. Classes $\mathbf{G}$ and $\mathbf{L}$
have $Q_L$ a perfect square (real branch points or no
branch points), so their Taylor coefficients have
constant sign and terminate finitely.
\end{remark}

\begin{proposition}[Shadow coefficient rationality;
\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{prop:shadow-coefficient-rationality}
\index{shadow coefficients!rationality|textbf}
\index{rationality!shadow tower}
For any chiral algebra with rational OPE structure
constants, every shadow coefficient $S_r(c)$ is a
rational function of the central charge~$c$. The
denominator has the form
$c^{r-3} \cdot (5c{+}22)^{\lfloor(r-2)/2\rfloor}$
\textup{(}up to a positive integer factor dividing~$r$\textup{)},
and the numerator is a polynomial of degree
$\lfloor(r{-}4)/2\rfloor$.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
The convolution recursion
$a_n = -(2c)^{-1}\sum_{j=1}^{n-1} a_j a_{n-j}$ with
$a_0 = c$, $a_1 = 6$, $a_2 = 40/(c(5c{+}22))$ produces
rational functions of~$c$ by induction: each step involves
only multiplication, addition, and division by~$2c$.
Since $S_r = a_{r-2}/r$, the rationality is preserved.
The denominator and numerator degree formulas are verified
through degree~$30$ by exact computation.
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Operadic complexity at degrees $5$--$7$]
\label{cor:operadic-complexity-5-7}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{operadic complexity!constructive verification at degrees 5--7}
\index{shadow obstruction tower!operadic complexity!degrees 5--7}
The operadic complexity identification
$r_{\max}(\cA) = f_\infty(\cA) = d_\infty(\cA)$
of Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:operadic-complexity-detailed}}
is verified constructively at degrees $5$, $6$, $7$:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item The degree-$k$ shadow coefficient $S_k$ coincides
 with the transferred $A_\infty$-operation
 $m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}$ on the primary line
 \textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:shadow-formality-higher-degree}(i)}\textup{)}.
\item The degree-$k$ shadow coefficient $S_k$ coincides
 with the evaluation of the transferred $L_\infty$
 bracket $\ell_k^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$ on the truncated
 MC element
 \textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:linfty-obstruction-5-6}(i)--(ii)}\textup{)}.
\item The vanishing/non-vanishing of $S_k$, $m_k^{\mathrm{tr}}$,
 and $\ell_k^{(0),\mathrm{tr}}$ agree for all four depth
 classes $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$
 at degrees $k = 5, 6, 7$.
\end{enumerate}
Together with the low-degree identification
\textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:shadow-formality-low-degree}},
degrees $2$--$4$\textup{)}, this gives the shadow--formality
identification constructively through degree~$7$: the
shadow obstruction tower, the $A_\infty$-formality obstruction tower,
and the $L_\infty$-formality obstruction tower coincide
through seven degrees, with the all-degree identification
proved non-constructively by induction
\textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification}}\textup{)}.

In the depth classification, this confirms that the
four-class partition
$\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$
is an operadic invariant: it measures the $L_\infty$-formality
level of $\gAmod$ simultaneously in the $A_\infty$
\textup{(}associative\textup{)} and $L_\infty$
\textup{(}Lie\textup{)} pictures. The agreement at
degrees $5$--$7$ for the Virasoro algebra is a nontrivial
check because the $A_\infty$ tree formula
\textup{(}$14 + 42 + 132$ trees at degrees $5$--$7$
via HPL\textup{)} and the $L_\infty$ stable-graph expansion
\textup{(}$105 + 945 + 10395$ trivalent trees at degrees
$5$--$7$\textup{)} count different combinatorial objects
yet produce the same scalar value $S_k$ on the primary
line.
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
Parts~(i) and~(ii) are immediate from
Propositions~\ref{prop:shadow-formality-higher-degree}
and~\ref{prop:linfty-obstruction-5-6} respectively.
Part~(iii) follows from the explicit verification tables in
those propositions: for classes $\mathbf{G}$,
$\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, all three quantities vanish at
degrees $5$, $6$, $7$ because the tower terminates
earlier. For class $\mathbf{M}$, all three are nonzero
because $\Delta \neq 0$ forces $S_k \neq 0$ for all
$k \geq 4$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:single-line-dichotomy}).

The tree count at degree~$7$ for the stable-graph route is
$(2 \cdot 8 - 5)!! = 11!! = 10395$: this is the number of
trivalent trees with $8$ external legs, which are the
fully resolved genus-$0$ stable graphs contributing to the
bracket $\ell_7^{(0)}$. The agreement with the HPL
route ($C_6 = 132$ planar trees) and the Feynman transform
route ($C_7 = 429$ binary trees on $8$ leaves) is a
consequence of the universal property
(Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-formality-identification},
Step~$3$).
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}[Shadow depth classification]
\label{def:shadow-depth-classification}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{shadow depth|textbf}
\index{shadow depth!classification}
\index{shadow obstruction tower!depth classification}
Let $\cA$ be a modular Koszul chiral algebra with
obstruction classes $o_r(\cA)$ from
Construction~\ref{constr:obstruction-recursion}.
Define the \emph{shadow depth}
$\kappa_d(\cA) := r_{\max}(\cA) =
\sup\{r \geq 2 : \cA^{\mathrm{sh}}_{r,0} \neq 0\}$
(with the convention $\kappa_d = \infty$ if the tower
does not terminate).
The \emph{shadow depth class} of~$\cA$ is determined
by the vanishing pattern of obstruction classes:
\begin{center}
\small
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}
\begin{tabular}{clll}
\toprule
\emph{Class} & \emph{Name} & $\kappa_d$ &
\emph{Intrinsic characterization} \\
\midrule
$\mathbf{G}$ & Gaussian &
 $2$ &
 \begin{minipage}[t]{0.42\textwidth}\raggedright
 $o_3(\cA) = 0$. Equivalently: $\gAmod$ is formal
 (all higher $L_\infty$ brackets transfer to zero).
 \end{minipage} \\[6pt]
$\mathbf{L}$ & Lie &
 $3$ &
 \begin{minipage}[t]{0.42\textwidth}\raggedright
 $o_3(\cA) \neq 0$ and $o_4(\cA) = 0$. Equivalently:
 $\gAmod$ is formal at ternary level; non-formality
 is exhausted by a single Massey product.
 \end{minipage} \\[6pt]
$\mathbf{C}$ & Contact &
 $4$ &
 \begin{minipage}[t]{0.42\textwidth}\raggedright
 $o_4(\cA) \neq 0$ and $o_5(\cA) = 0$. Equivalently:
 non-formality persists through the quartic stage
 but is killed by a rigidity constraint at degree~$5$.
 \end{minipage} \\[6pt]
$\mathbf{M}$ & Mixed &
 $\infty$ &
 \begin{minipage}[t]{0.42\textwidth}\raggedright
 $o_r(\cA) \neq 0$ for infinitely many~$r$.
 Equivalently: $\gAmod$ is intrinsically non-formal.
 \end{minipage} \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
These four classes are exhaustive and mutually exclusive
for the standard families at low rank. At higher rank,
\emph{finite depths $d \geq 5$} are realized by lattice vertex
algebras whose theta functions involve cusp forms
(Chapter~\ref{chap:arithmetic-shadows},
\S\ref{sec:higher-depths}): depth~$d$ requires a lattice
of rank~$r$ with $\dim S_{r/2}(\Gamma) \geq d-3$, and
each independent cusp form contributes one critical line
of the constrained Epstein zeta. Even unimodular lattices
of ranks $48, 72, 96, \dotsc$ achieve depths
$5, 6, 7,\dotsc$\,, with the depth growing as the
number of cusp forms in $M_{r/2}$ grows. The four classes
$\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ thus
form a \emph{coarse} classification; the fine classification
is the shadow depth $d \in \{2,3,4,5,\dotsc\} \cup \{\infty\}$,
and every finite value is realized.

The classification presupposes chiral Koszulness. Beyond the Koszul
boundary lies a fifth regime: class~$\mathbf{W}$ (wild), in which
the bar spectral sequence does not collapse at any finite page and
the shadow depth is undefined
(Remark~\ref{rem:wild-quiver-boundary}).
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[The Koszul boundary and class~$\mathbf{W}$]
\label{rem:wild-quiver-boundary}
\index{shadow depth!wild class}%
\index{wild quiver!Koszul boundary}%
\index{Kronecker quiver!Koszul boundary}%
The Kronecker quiver $K_m$ (two vertices, $m$ parallel arrows)
exhibits a sharp Koszul boundary detected by the sign of the
determinant of the symmetric Euler form:
$\det \langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle_{\mathrm{sym}} = 4 - m^2$.
For $m \leq 2$ the form is positive semi-definite, the bar
spectral sequence collapses at~$E_2$, and the standard shadow
depth classification applies: $K_1$ is class~$\mathbf{L}$
(finite type $A_2$, shadow depth~$3$) and $K_2$ is class~$\mathbf{M}$
(tame type $\widehat{A}_1$, infinite shadow depth). For $m \geq 3$ the
form is indefinite: the signed Euler series
$\prod_{n \geq 1}(1 - t^n)^{m+2}$ acquires negative coefficients
(first at weight~$2$), obstructing bar concentration and hence
Koszulness. The shadow obstruction tower $\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$
cannot be constructed, and the role of the shadow invariants is
taken by the Donaldson--Thomas invariants $\Omega(d_0,d_1)$ of
Kontsevich--Soibelman, which grow exponentially in the wild
regime. We denote this regime class~$\mathbf{W}$ (wild):
the extended classification is
$\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}/\mathbf{W}$,
with $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ exhaustive
within the Koszul world and $\mathbf{W}$ marking its complement.

Computational verification:
\texttt{compute/lib/wild\_quiver\_chiral\_engine.py} (89~tests).
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Splitting table of the shadow Galois extension]%
\label{rem:shadow-galois-splitting}%
\index{shadow field!Galois extension}%
\index{shadow depth!Galois interpretation}%
The depth classification
$\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$
is the splitting table of the shadow Galois extension
$K_L/F$, where $F = k(c)$ is the field of
central-charge-rational functions and
$K_L = F(\sqrt{Q_L})$ is the extension by the
square root of the shadow metric.
Class~$\mathbf{G}$: $Q_L$ is a constant square,
$K_L = F$ (split).
Class~$\mathbf{L}$: $Q_L$ is a nonconstant square,
$K_L = F$ (split, nontrivially).
Class~$\mathbf{M}$: $Q_L$ is irreducible,
$[K_L : F] = 2$ (inert).
The depth is the ramification index of $\sqrt{Q_L}$ over
the base field.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[K-theoretic interpretation of the depth classification]
\label{rem:bott-periodicity-dichotomy}
\index{shadow depth!K-theoretic interpretation}
\index{Bott periodicity!shadow depth dichotomy}
The critical discriminant $\Delta = 0$ characterizes exact Bott
periodicity of the shadow generating function: $\Delta = 0$ if and
only if the shadow tower terminates, if and only if the associated
K-theory class is periodic. For class~$\mathbf{M}$
($\Delta \neq 0$), the Koszul monodromy $-1$ at the zeros of~$Q_L$
produces $\bZ/2$-torsion in the associated K-group.
\end{remark}


\begin{remark}[Shadow tower as Taylor expansion of the MC infinity-groupoid]
\label{rem:mc-infinity-groupoid-taylor}
\ClaimStatusHeuristic
\index{shadow obstruction tower!MC infinity-groupoid}%
\index{Maurer--Cartan space!shadow tower as Taylor expansion}%
\index{shadow depth!MC moduli interpretation}%
The shadow invariants $\kappa, C, Q, \dotsc$ admit a
homotopy-theoretic reading: they are the Taylor coefficients of
the formal expansion of the Maurer--Cartan space
$\operatorname{MC}(\gAmod)$, viewed as an infinity-groupoid,
around the bar-intrinsic element
$\Theta_{\cA}$ of Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}.
The degree-$r$ shadow $S_r(\cA)$ is the $r$-jet of the
formal neighbourhood of $\Theta_{\cA}$ in this MC space;
the shadow depth $r_{\max}$ records whether the formal
neighbourhood is polynomial (finite jet) or requires
the full power series.

Under this reading, the four depth classes acquire a
topological interpretation:
\begin{itemize}
\item Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($r_{\max} = 2$):
 the formal neighbourhood is quadratic.
 The MC space near $\Theta_{\cA}$ is contractible;
 all higher homotopy groups vanish.
 The shadow tower is a single Gaussian integral.
\item Class~$\mathbf{L}$ ($r_{\max} = 3$):
 the formal neighbourhood is cubic.
 The MC space has the homotopy type of a
 disjoint union of contractible components, each
 indexed by a single Massey product.
 The sole obstruction $\ell_3^{\mathrm{sh}}$ is the
 classifying map of the unique nontrivial fibration.
\item Class~$\mathbf{C}$ ($r_{\max} = 4$):
 the formal neighbourhood is quartic.
 The MC space carries nontrivial but finitely generated
 homotopy; rigidity at degree~$5$ kills all further
 obstructions.
\item Class~$\mathbf{M}$ ($r_{\max} = \infty$):
 no finite jet suffices.
 The MC space has the homotopy type of an
 infinite Postnikov tower: every stage
 $\Theta_{\cA}^{\leq r}$ is a genuine truncation,
 and the passage $r \to r+1$ introduces new homotopy.
 The discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4 \neq 0$ is the
 obstruction to collapsing the tower at the first
 nontrivial stage.
\end{itemize}
The finiteness dichotomy ($\Delta = 0$ vs.\ $\Delta \neq 0$)
thus separates algebras whose MC space is a finite CW~complex
(classes $\mathbf{G}$, $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$) from those
whose MC space is an inverse limit of finite stages
(class~$\mathbf{M}$).
This is consistent with the Galois interpretation of
Remark~\ref{rem:shadow-galois-splitting}: the splitting of the
shadow field extension $K_L/F$ reflects the collapsing of the
Postnikov tower, and the inertness of $K_L$ for class~$\mathbf{M}$
reflects the infinite homotopy type.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Shadow depth as BPS complexity]
\label{rem:shadow-depth-bps}
\index{shadow depth!BPS complexity}%
\index{BPS particles!shadow depth classification}%
In the infrared decomposition of a $4$d $\cN = 2$ theory,
the Coulomb branch produces $U(1)^r$ (the abelian/Gaussian sector)
and BPS particles produce the interacting corrections.
The shadow depth classification reflects this decomposition:
\begin{itemize}
\item Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($r_{\max} = 2$): pure abelian, no BPS
 particles. The shadow tower terminates because there are no
 interactions beyond the free-field determinant.
\item Class~$\mathbf{L}$ ($r_{\max} = 3$): linear BPS sector.
 A single cubic interaction (the Lie bracket), killed by the
 Jacobi identity at the next order.
\item Class~$\mathbf{C}$ ($r_{\max} = 4$): quadratic BPS.
 The quartic contact term from composite fields terminates
 by stratum separation (rank-one rigidity).
\item Class~$\mathbf{M}$ ($r_{\max} = \infty$): all-order BPS.
 The infinite $A_\infty$ tower reflects a BPS sector with
 corrections at every order, characteristic of theories with
 gravitational content (Virasoro, $\cW_N$).
\end{itemize}
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Shadow depth and the Costello--Dimofte K-matrix]
\label{rem:shadow-depth-k-matrix}
\index{shadow depth!K-matrix}%
\index{Costello--Dimofte!K-matrix}%
In the Costello--Dimofte--Gaiotto framework for $3$d HT
theories~\cite{CDG2024}, line operators carry a modified
coproduct $\Delta_K = K \circ \Delta$, where the K-matrix~$K$
measures the failure of the naive coproduct to intertwine the
$R$-matrix action. The shadow depth~$r_{\max}$ controls the
complexity of~$K$: for class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($r_{\max} = 2$),
$K = \mathrm{id}$ and the coproduct is primitive; for
class~$\mathbf{L}$ ($r_{\max} = 3$), $K$ is a single Lie-bracket
correction; for class~$\mathbf{M}$ ($r_{\max} = \infty$), $K$
receives corrections at every order in the $A_\infty$
tower. The shadow obstruction tower
$\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ is the generating function for the
K-matrix corrections at each degree: the quartic resonance
class~$\mathfrak{Q}$ at $r = 4$ is the first correction
to the Yangian coproduct that cannot be removed by gauge
transformation.
\end{remark}

\begin{example}[Shadow depth of all computed families]
\label{ex:shadow-depth-all-families}
\index{shadow depth!computed families}
\begin{center}
\small
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}
\begin{tabular}{llcccl}
\toprule
\emph{Family} &
\emph{Class} &
$d$ &
$d_{\mathrm{alg}}$ &
$\Delta$ &
\emph{Reference} \\
\midrule
Heisenberg $\cH_k$ &
 $\mathbf{G}$ & $2$ & $0$ & $0$ &
 \S\ref{sec:heisenberg-shadow-gaussianity} \\
Lattice VOA $V_\Lambda$ &
 $\mathbf{G}$ & $2$ & $0$ & $0$ &
 Thm~\ref{thm:lattice:curvature-braiding-orthogonal} \\
Free fermion &
 $\mathbf{G}$ & $2$ & $0$ & $0$ &
 \S\ref{sec:heisenberg-shadow-gaussianity} \\[3pt]
$\hat{\fg}_k$ (generic) &
 $\mathbf{L}$ & $3$ & $1$ & $0$ &
 \S\ref{sec:affine-cubic-shadow} \\[3pt]
$\beta\gamma$ &
 $\mathbf{C}$ & $4$ & $2$ & \textsuperscript{$\ast$} &
 Thm~\ref{thm:betagamma-global-depth} \\[3pt]
$\mathrm{Vir}_c$ &
 $\mathbf{M}$ & $\infty$ & $\infty$
 & $\frac{40}{5c{+}22}$ &
 Thm~\ref{thm:w-virasoro-quintic-forced} \\
$\mathcal{W}_N$ &

 & $\neq 0$ &
 \S\ref{sec:mixed-cubic-quartic-shadows} \\[6pt]
\multicolumn{6}{l}{\emph{Higher-rank lattice VOAs
 (\S\ref{sec:higher-depths}; class by $d_{\mathrm{alg}}$, cf.\
 Theorem~\ref{thm:depth-decomposition}):}} \\
$V_{\mathrm{Leech}}$ &
 $\mathbf{G}$ & $4$ & $0$ & $0$ &
 Prop~\ref{prop:leech-epstein} \\
Rank-$48$ &
 $\mathbf{G}$ & $5$ & $0$ & $0$ &

Rank-$72$ &
 $\mathbf{G}$ & $6$ & $0$ & $0$ &
 \S\ref{sec:higher-depths} \\
\bottomrule
\multicolumn{6}{l}{\textsuperscript{$\ast$}%
 Stratum-separated global family:
 $S_2 = 6\lambda^2 - 6\lambda + 1$, $S_3 = 0$,
 $S_4 = -5/12$, $S_r = 0$ for $r \geq 5$.
 The charged quartic lies off the $T$-line, so the single-line
 discriminant is not the class-$\mathbf{C}$ witness.}
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
\end{example}

\begin{theorem}[Shadow archetype classification]
\label{thm:shadow-archetype-classification}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{shadow depth!classification theorem|textbf}
\index{shadow archetype classification|textbf}
Every chirally Koszul algebra in the standard landscape has algebraic
shadow depth
\[
 d_{\mathrm{alg}}(\cA) \in \{0,\, 1,\, 2,\, \infty\}.
\]
Equivalently, it belongs to exactly one of the shadow depth classes
$\mathbf{G}$, $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, or $\mathbf{M}$
\textup{(}Definition~\textup{\ref{def:shadow-depth-classification}}).
The class is determined by the vanishing pattern of obstruction
classes at degrees~$3$, $4$, $5$, and the assignments for the
primitive archetypes are as follows:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item \emph{Gaussian \textup{(}$\mathbf{G}$,
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 0$, $r_{\max} = 2$\textup{)}:}
 Heisenberg $\cH_c$, lattice VOA $V_\Lambda$, free fermion.
 All higher obstruction classes vanish:
 $o_r(\cA) = 0$ for $r \geq 3$. The complementarity potential is
 exactly quadratic.

\item \emph{Lie \textup{(}$\mathbf{L}$,
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 1$, $r_{\max} = 3$\textup{)}:}
 Affine Kac--Moody $V_k(\fg)$ at generic level.
 The cubic shadow $\mathfrak{C}_{\mathrm{aff}}(x,y,z) =
 \kappa(x,[y,z])$ is the unique non-vanishing higher shadow; the
 quartic obstruction $o_4 = 0$ by the Jacobi identity.

\item \emph{Contact \textup{(}$\mathbf{C}$,
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 2$, $r_{\max} = 4$\textup{)}:}
 the standard conformal-weight family $\beta\gamma_\lambda$
 \textup{(}and its $bc$ dual\textup{)}.
 The class-$\mathbf{C}$ witness is not the internal weight-changing
 line and not the $T$-line: on the standard $\lambda$-family one has
 \[
  S_2 = 6\lambda^2 - 6\lambda + 1,\qquad
  S_3 = 0,\qquad
  S_4 = -5/12,\qquad
  S_r = 0 \quad (r \geq 5).
 \]
 Thus the charged quartic contact class is nonzero and is the last
 non-vanishing shadow component; the quintic obstruction vanishes by
 rank-one abelian rigidity and stratum separation
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:betagamma-global-depth}};
 Corollary~\textup{\ref{cor:nms-betagamma-mu-vanishing}};
 Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:contact-stratum-separation}}\textup{)}.

\item \emph{Mixed \textup{(}$\mathbf{M}$,
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = \infty$, $r_{\max} = \infty$\textup{)}:}
 Virasoro $\mathrm{Vir}_c$, $\mathcal{W}_N$ ($N \geq 3$).
 The cubic shadow $\mathfrak{C}_{\mathrm{Vir}} = 2x^3$ and quartic
 shadow $\mathfrak{Q}^{\mathrm{ct}}_{\mathrm{Vir}} =
 \tfrac{10}{c(5c{+}22)}\,x^4$ are both non-vanishing, and
 $o_5(\mathrm{Vir}) = \{\mathfrak{C}, \mathfrak{Q}\}_{H} \neq 0$
 for generic~$c$
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:w-virasoro-quintic-forced}}).
 The tower is infinite by induction on the all-degree master equation.
 For multi-generator algebras $\mathcal{W}_N$ ($N \geq 3$),
 diagonal non-autonomy at degree~$6$ is governed by the
 propagator variance
 \textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:propagator-variance}}):
 it vanishes at isolated curvature-proportionality loci in the
 central charge.
\end{enumerate}
Shadow depth is orthogonal to Koszulness: all archetypes are
chirally Koszul
\textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:modular-koszul-duality-main}}),
yet their shadow depths differ
\textup{(}Figure~\textup{\ref{fig:shadow-archetype-classification})}.
The shadow depth classifies the
complexity of $\Theta_\cA$, not whether the bar complex is acyclic.

For lattice vertex algebras, the shadow--spectral correspondence
\textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shadow-spectral-correspondence}})
identifies the total shadow depth with $1$ plus the number of critical
lines of the constrained Epstein zeta. The total depth is unbounded:
every finite value $d \geq 2$ is realized by even unimodular
lattices of sufficiently high rank, with depths $5, 6, 7, \dotsc$
arising from independent cusp forms in $M_{r/2}$
\textup{(}\S\textup{\ref{sec:higher-depths})}. This does not create
new algebraic-shadow archetypes: for lattices one has
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 0$, so the extra depth is purely arithmetic.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Each case follows from the established vanishing theorems:

(i)~For Heisenberg and lattice VOAs, the bar complex is strictly
coassociative ($m_k = 0$ for $k \geq 3$ in the transferred
$A_\infty$-structure), so all obstruction sources vanish
(Theorem~\ref{thm:nms-heisenberg-exact-linearity}).

(ii)~For affine algebras, the cubic shadow is the Lie bracket
transported through the bar complex
(Theorem~\ref{thm:nms-affine-cubic-normal-form}). The quartic
obstruction $o_4 = \tfrac{1}{2}\{\mathfrak{C},\mathfrak{C}\}_H$
vanishes by the Jacobi identity: the cyclic double Lie bracket
$\kappa([x,[y,z]],w)$ summed over cyclic permutations is zero.

(iii)~For $\beta\gamma_\lambda$, the weight-changing line alone is not
the class-$\mathbf{C}$ witness: its shadow tower vanishes and
$\mu_{\beta\gamma} = 0$ by rank-one abelian rigidity
\textup{(}Corollary~\textup{\ref{cor:nms-betagamma-mu-vanishing}}\textup{)}.
The $T$-line is also not the class-$\mathbf{C}$ witness:
it has $S_3 = 2$, $S_4 = 10/[c(5c{+}22)]$, and infinite shadow depth
\textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:betagamma-T-line-shadows}}\textup{)}.
The class-$\mathbf{C}$ witness is the standard conformal-weight family
$\beta\gamma_\lambda$:
there the cubic shadow vanishes, the charged quartic survives with
$S_4 = -5/12$, and Theorem~\ref{thm:betagamma-global-depth} gives
$S_r = 0$ for $r \geq 5$. Hence $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 2$ and
$r_{\max} = 4$.

(iv)~For Virasoro, the cubic and quartic shadows coexist, and their
Poisson bracket $\{\mathfrak{C}, \mathfrak{Q}\}_H$ is non-zero for
generic $c$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:w-virasoro-quintic-forced}); the all-degree master
equation (Theorem~\ref{thm:nms-all-degree-master-equation}) then
forces all higher obstructions to be non-zero by induction.

That all four families are Koszul follows from
Proposition~\ref{prop:pbw-universality} (PBW implies Koszulness)
for affine and W-algebras, Theorem~\ref{thm:heisenberg-koszul-dual-early}
for Heisenberg, Theorem~\ref{thm:lattice:koszul-morphism} for lattice
VOAs, and Theorem~\ref{thm:betagamma-bar-cohomology} for $\beta\gamma$.
\end{proof}

%
% L column: cubic then stops:
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (1,3.8) {$\kappa$};
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (1,2.9) {$\mathfrak{C} = f^{abc}$};
\node[font=\scriptsize, text=black!35] at (1,2.0) {$0$ (Jacobi)};
\node[font=\scriptsize, text=black!35] at (1,1.1) {$0$};
\node[font=\scriptsize, text=black!35] at (1,0.2) {$0$};
\draw[thick, black!40] (0.65,2.4) -- (1.35,2.4);
\node[font=\tiny, text=black!50] at (1,2.15) {\textsf{terminates}};
%
% C column: quartic then stops:
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (2,3.8) {$\kappa$};
\node[font=\scriptsize, text=black!35] at (2,2.9) {$0$ (abelian)};
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (2,2.0) {$\mathfrak{Q} = \mathrm{cyc}(m_3)$};
\node[font=\scriptsize, text=black!35] at (2,1.1) {$0$ (rank $1$)};
\node[font=\scriptsize, text=black!35] at (2,0.2) {$0$};
\draw[thick, black!40] (1.65,1.5) -- (2.35,1.5);
\node[font=\tiny, text=black!50] at (2,1.25) {\textsf{terminates}};
%
% M column: infinite tower:
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (3,3.8) {$\kappa$};
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (3,2.9) {$\mathfrak{C} = 2x^3$};
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (3,2.0)
 {$\mathfrak{Q}^{\mathrm{ct}} = \tfrac{10}{c(5c+22)}x^4$};
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (3,1.1)
 {$o_5 = \{\mathfrak{C},\mathfrak{Q}\}_H \neq 0$};
\node[font=\scriptsize] at (3,0.2) {$o_r \neq 0\;\forall\,r$};
\node[font=\tiny, text=black!50] at (3,-0.2) {\textsf{infinite}};
%
% Horizontal rule below headers:
\draw[black!25] (-0.5,4.3) -- (3.5,4.3);
%
% Complexity arrow:
\draw[maparrow, black!40] (-0.45,-0.5) -- (3.45,-0.5);
\node[font=\tiny\scshape, text=black!50, anchor=north] at (1.5,-0.55)
 {increasing shadow complexity};
\end{tikzpicture}
\caption{The four shadow archetypes.
Each column shows the non-vanishing shadows at each degree
for the primitive representative of the class.
Class~$\mathbf{G}$ is purely scalar ($\kappa$ only);
class~$\mathbf{L}$ adds the Lie cubic shadow;
class~$\mathbf{C}$ has a quartic contact invariant
but no cubic;
class~$\mathbf{M}$ has all obstructions non-vanishing,
giving an infinite shadow obstruction tower.
All four classes are chirally Koszul; shadow depth
classifies the complexity of $\Theta_\cA$,
not whether the bar complex is acyclic.}%
\label{fig:shadow-archetype-classification}
\end{figure}

\begin{corollary}[Shadow depth under Koszul duality]
\label{cor:shadow-depth-koszul-invariance}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{shadow depth!Koszul duality invariance}
\index{Koszul duality!preserves shadow depth}
Koszul duality preserves shadow depth:
$r_{\max}(\cA^!) = r_{\max}(\cA)$. In particular, the shadow
class $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ is an
invariant of the Koszul duality orbit.
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
By Theorem~\ref{thm:modular-koszul-duality-main}(iv),
$\mathbb{D}(\Theta_\cA) = \Theta_{\cA^!}$, where
$\mathbb{D}$ is the Verdier involution. Since $\mathbb{D}$
is an automorphism of~$\gAmod$ respecting the weight
filtration, it bijects the obstruction classes:
$o_r(\cA) = 0$ if and only if $o_r(\cA^!) = 0$.
Hence $r_{\max}(\cA) = r_{\max}(\cA^!)$.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Shadow depth refines GKW formality]
\label{rem:shadow-depth-gkw-refinement}
\index{GKW formality!shadow depth refinement}
\index{Gaiotto--Kulp--Wu|see{GKW formality}}
\index{shadow depth!GKW refinement}
The $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ classification
is a strict four-class refinement of the binary formal/non-formal
dichotomy of Gaiotto--Kulp--Wu~\cite{GKW2025}.
In their framework, a theory with topological direction
$d' \geq 2$ is formal (all higher operations vanish),
while $d' = 1$ theories are generically non-formal.
The shadow depth resolves the $d' = 1$ non-formal class
into three distinct strata:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item Class~$\mathbf{G}$ is formal \emph{even at}
 $d' = 1$: the OPE is abelian, so all transferred
 $A_\infty$-operations $m_k^{\mathrm{SC}} = 0$ for
 $k \geq 3$ (Heisenberg, lattice VOAs).
 This class is strictly larger than the GKW formal locus.
\item Class~$\mathbf{L}$: $m_3 \neq 0$ from the Lie bracket,
 $m_k = 0$ for $k \geq 4$ (Jacobi identity kills the
 quartic obstruction).
\item Class~$\mathbf{C}$: $m_3 = 0$ on the neutral stratum,
 $m_4 \neq 0$ from the contact quartic, $m_k = 0$ for
 $k \geq 5$ (rank-one rigidity).
\item Class~$\mathbf{M}$: $m_k \neq 0$ for all $k \geq 3$
 (the all-degree master equation forces an infinite tower).
\end{enumerate}
The map $\varphi\colon
\{\mathbf{G},\mathbf{L},\mathbf{C},\mathbf{M}\}
\to \{\text{formal}, \text{non-formal}\}$
sending $\mathbf{G} \mapsto \text{formal}$ and
$\{\mathbf{L},\mathbf{C},\mathbf{M}\} \mapsto \text{non-formal}$
is surjective but not injective: three classes collapse
to one in the GKW picture.
The shadow metric discriminant
$\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$
(Definition~\ref{def:shadow-metric})
is the quantitative invariant that GKW's binary
classification does not capture:
$\Delta = 0$ separates finite shadow depth
($\mathbf{G}$/$\mathbf{L}$) from infinite shadow depth
($\mathbf{M}$), while the vanishing pattern of
$\alpha$ and $S_4$ individually distinguishes
$\mathbf{G}$ from $\mathbf{L}$ and $\mathbf{C}$
from~$\mathbf{M}$.
The GKW formality/non-formality dichotomy is the genus-$0$,
degree-$3$ projection of the shadow depth classification:
it detects whether the cubic Swiss-cheese operation $m_3^{\mathrm{SC}}$
vanishes, which separates class~$\mathbf{G}$ from
$\{\mathbf{L}, \mathbf{C}, \mathbf{M}\}$.
The shadow obstruction tower extends this to all genera and degrees:
the quartic discriminant $\Delta$ distinguishes $\mathbf{C}$
from~$\mathbf{M}$, and the all-genera MC equation
$D \cdot \Theta + \tfrac{1}{2}[\Theta, \Theta] = 0$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic})
governs the complete tower from which GKW's consistency
conditions are the genus-$0$ projections.
Computational verification:
\texttt{compute/lib/theorem\_shadow\_depth\_gkw\_engine.py}
($78$~tests).
\end{remark}

\begin{proposition}[Swiss-cheese formality classification by shadow class;
\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{prop:sc-formality-by-class}
\index{Swiss-cheese!formality classification|textbf}
\index{shadow depth!Swiss-cheese formality}
Three distinct formality properties are governed by the
shadow depth class. Let $\cA$ be a modular Koszul chiral
algebra.
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item \emph{$A_\infty$-formality of bar cohomology}
 \textup{(}operations on $H^*(\Barch(\cA))$\textup{)}:
 the transferred $A_\infty$ operations $m_k = 0$ for
 $k \geq 3$. This holds for \emph{all} Koszul algebras in
 \emph{all} four classes $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:koszul-equivalences-meta}},
 item~\textup{(iii))}.
\item \emph{Swiss-cheese operations $m_k^{\mathrm{SC}}$ on
 $\cA$ itself} \textup{(}encoded by the
 $\mathrm{SC}^{\mathrm{ch,top}}$ operad\textup{)}:
 \begin{center}
 \small
 \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}
 \begin{tabular}{clcl}
 \toprule
 \emph{Class} & \emph{SC formality} &
 $m_k^{\mathrm{SC}}$ for $k \geq 3$ &
 \emph{Mechanism} \\
 \midrule
 $\mathbf{G}$ & formal &
 all zero & abelian OPE \\
 $\mathbf{L}$ & non-formal at $k{=}3$ &
 $m_3^{\mathrm{SC}}{\neq}0$, $m_k^{\mathrm{SC}}{=}0$ for $k \geq 4$ & Jacobi cubic; quartic killed \\
 $\mathbf{C}$ & non-formal at $k{=}4$ &
 $m_3^{\mathrm{SC}}{=}0$, $m_4^{\mathrm{SC}}{\neq}0$ &
 contact quartic, rank-$1$ rigidity \\
 $\mathbf{M}$ & fully non-formal &
 all nonzero & infinite shadow tower \\
 \bottomrule
 \end{tabular}
 \end{center}
\item \emph{$L_\infty$ brackets $\ell_k$ on the modular
 convolution algebra $\gAmod$}:
 $\ell_3 \neq 0$ for classes
 $\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$
 \textup{(}the cubic shadow
 $\mathfrak{C}(\cA) \neq 0$\textup{)};
 $\ell_k \neq 0$ for all $k \geq 3$ in class~$\mathbf{M}$.
 Class~$\mathbf{G}$ has $\ell_k = 0$ for all
 $k \geq 3$.
\end{enumerate}
The three aspects are logically independent\textup{:}
aspect~\textup{(i)} holds universally on the Koszul locus,
while aspects~\textup{(ii)} and~\textup{(iii)} detect the
shadow depth class. The common notation~``$m_k$'' for both
\textup{(i)} and~\textup{(ii)} is a source of systematic
confusion (i)~lives on $H^*(\Barch)$,
(ii)~lives on~$\cA$, and (iii)~lives on~$\gAmod$.
Computational verification:
\texttt{compute/lib/theorem\_ainfty\_nonformality\_class\_m\_engine.py}.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Aspect~(i): Theorem~\ref{thm:koszul-equivalences-meta}(iii).
Aspect~(ii): for class~$\mathbf{G}$,
the SC operations on~$\cA$ vanish for $k \geq 3$ because
Heisenberg has trivial OPE beyond the Killing form.
For class~$\mathbf{L}$ (affine KM), $m_3^{\mathrm{SC}} \neq 0$
from the Jacobi cubic tree sum, but
$m_k^{\mathrm{SC}} = 0$ for $k \geq 4$ because the Jacobi
identity kills the quartic contact
(Proposition~\ref{prop:swiss-cheese-nonformality-by-class}).
For class~$\mathbf{C}$
($\beta\gamma$), $m_3^{\mathrm{SC}} = 0$ on the neutral
stratum and $m_4^{\mathrm{SC}} \neq 0$ from the contact
quartic invariant $Q^{\mathrm{contact}}$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:betagamma-quartic-birth}).
For class~$\mathbf{M}$, the quintic-forced theorem
(Theorem~\ref{thm:w-virasoro-quintic-forced})
proves all $m_k^{\mathrm{SC}} \neq 0$
(Virasoro: infinite pole tower drives all degrees).
Aspect~(iii):
$\ell_k = S_k(\cA)$ is the shadow obstruction tower
coefficient at degree~$k$, which is nonzero for
$k \leq r_{\max}(\cA)$
(Definition~\ref{def:shadow-depth-classification}).
\end{proof}

% ================================================================
% Shadow depth escalator
% ================================================================

\begin{proposition}[Shadow depth escalator; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{prop:shadow-depth-escalator}
\index{shadow depth!escalator|textbf}
\index{Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction!shadow depth escalator|textbf}
Current-algebra systems (affine Kac--Moody) are class~$\mathbf{L}$:
their shadow obstruction towers terminate at degree~$3$, and the
complementarity sum vanishes ($\kappa(\cA) + \kappa(\cA^!) = 0$).
W-algebra systems (Virasoro, $\cW_N$) are class~$\mathbf{M}$:
their towers are infinite, and $\kappa + \kappa' \neq 0$
\textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:ds-complementarity-tower-main})}.
Principal Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction is the functor that sends
$\mathbf{L}$ to~$\mathbf{M}$. Three mechanisms operate
simultaneously:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item \emph{Cubic shadow doubling.}
 Affine $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_N$ has cubic shadow
 $S_3^{\mathrm{KM}}$; the reduced $\cW_N$ has
 $S_3^{\cW} = 2\,S_3^{\mathrm{KM}}$
 \textup{(}universal for principal reduction at all~$N$,
 Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:ds-package-functoriality}(i))}.

\item \emph{Quartic creation from zero.}
 The Jacobi identity kills the quartic invariant for affine
 algebras: $S_4^{\mathrm{KM}} = 0$, hence $\Delta = 0$, hence
 class~$\mathbf{L}$. The BRST differential creates a nonzero
 quartic on the quotient: $S_4^{\cW} \neq 0$, $\Delta \neq 0$,
 hence class~$\mathbf{M}$. Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction does
 not modify the quartic invariant; it creates it from nothing.

\item \emph{Growth-rate improvement.}
 The convergence radius of the shadow obstruction tower increases
 under reduction: at $N = 2$, $\rho(13) \approx 0.467$ for the
 Virasoro; at $N = 3$, $\rho$ is smaller. The tower becomes
 better-behaved as the algebra becomes more complicated.
\end{enumerate}
The shadow depth classification is a property of the algebra
\emph{relative to its pre-reduction ancestor}: Drinfeld--Sokolov
reduction is the escalator that lifts the tower from finite to
infinite.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
(i)~Follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:ds-package-functoriality}(i):
the anomaly ratio $\varrho(\mathfrak{g})$ of the reduction
governs the transformation of the cubic shadow.
(ii)~The quartic shadow $S_4^{\mathrm{KM}} = 0$ by the Jacobi
identity (proof of
Theorem~\ref{thm:modular-koszul-duality-main}(ii)).
For the reduced algebra $\cW^k(\mathfrak{g})$, the BRST
differential introduces new composite fields whose OPE
generates a nonzero quartic contact invariant
$Q^{\mathrm{contact}}_{\cW} \neq 0$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:nms-betagamma-quartic-birth} for the
$\beta\gamma$ case; for $\cW_N$,
Theorem~\ref{thm:wn-obstruction}).
(iii)~The convergence radius $R = 2\kappa/\sqrt{9\alpha^2 + 2\Delta}$
increases with~$N$ because $\kappa$ grows as
$\varrho(\mathfrak{sl}_N)\cdot c$ while the shadow coefficients
grow more slowly.
\end{proof}


\begin{conjecture}[Shadow depth monotonicity under Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction]
\label{conj:ds-shadow-depth-monotonicity}
\ClaimStatusConjectured
\index{shadow depth!monotonicity under DS reduction}%
\index{Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction!shadow depth monotonicity}%
Let $\mathfrak{g}$ be a finite-dimensional simple Lie algebra,
$f \in \mathfrak{g}$ a non-zero nilpotent element, and
$k \neq -h^\vee$ a non-critical level.
Write $H^0_{\mathrm{DS}}(V_k(\mathfrak{g}), f)
= \mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak{g}, f)$ for the quantum
Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction at~$f$. Then
\[
r_{\max}\bigl(\mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak{g}, f)\bigr)
\;\geq\;
r_{\max}\bigl(V_k(\mathfrak{g})\bigr)
\;=\; 3.
\]
The inequality is strict for every non-zero nilpotent:
$r_{\max}(\mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak{g}, f)) = \infty$
\textup{(}class~$\mathbf{M}$\textup{)} whenever $f \neq 0$.

Equivalently: the only shadow depth class realized by an
affine Kac--Moody algebra is~$\mathbf{L}$, and every
non-trivial DS reduction escalates the shadow depth
from finite to infinite.
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[Evidence]
\label{rem:ds-monotonicity-evidence}
\index{shadow depth!monotonicity evidence}%
Three independent lines of evidence support
Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:ds-shadow-depth-monotonicity}}.

\emph{(a)~Principal reductions.}
Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:shadow-depth-escalator}} proves
the conjecture for the principal nilpotent $f_{\mathrm{prin}}$
in every simple~$\mathfrak{g}$: the Sugawara construction
escalates the OPE pole order from~$2$ \textup{(}affine\textup{)} to~$4$
\textup{(}Virasoro subalgebra\textup{)}, creating
$S_4 \neq 0$ and hence $\Delta \neq 0$.

\emph{(b)~Non-principal reductions in type~$A$.}
Systematic computation
\textup{(}\texttt{compute/lib/ds\_nonprincipal\_shadows.py},
function~\texttt{orbit\_shadow\_hierarchy}\textup{)}
verifies the conjecture for all nilpotent orbits of
$\mathfrak{sl}_N$ ($N = 3, 4, 5$) at multiple levels:
\begin{center}
\small
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.1}
\begin{tabular}{llcc}
\toprule
$\mathfrak{g}$ & Partition & Nilpotent type & Shadow class \\
\midrule
$\mathfrak{sl}_3$ & $(1^3)$ & trivial & $\mathbf{L}$ \\
$\mathfrak{sl}_3$ & $(2,1)$ & subregular (BP) & $\mathbf{M}$ \\
$\mathfrak{sl}_3$ & $(3)$ & principal & $\mathbf{M}$ \\[3pt]
$\mathfrak{sl}_4$ & $(1^4)$ & trivial & $\mathbf{L}$ \\
$\mathfrak{sl}_4$ & $(2,1^2)$ & minimal & $\mathbf{M}$ \\
$\mathfrak{sl}_4$ & $(2^2)$ & rectangular & $\mathbf{M}$ \\
$\mathfrak{sl}_4$ & $(3,1)$ & hook & $\mathbf{M}$ \\
$\mathfrak{sl}_4$ & $(4)$ & principal & $\mathbf{M}$ \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
The pattern is level-independent: verified at
$k = -1, 1, 3, 5, 10$ for each orbit.

\emph{(c)~Mechanism.}
Every non-trivial nilpotent~$f$ produces, via the BRST
construction, at least one composite strong generator whose
self-OPE has pole order $\geq 4$.
For the principal nilpotent, this is the Sugawara stress
tensor $T = (2(k+h^\vee))^{-1} \sum_a {:}J^a J_a{:}$ with
$T(z)T(w) \sim (c/2)(z{-}w)^{-4} + \dotsb$\,.
For a non-principal nilpotent of Jordan type~$\lambda$,
the reduced algebra $\mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak{g}, f_\lambda)$
retains a Virasoro subalgebra with the same quartic-pole
mechanism, producing $S_4 \neq 0$ on the $T$-line.
The conjecture would follow from establishing that the
Virasoro subalgebra of every non-trivial DS reduction
has $c \neq 0$ and $5c + 22 \neq 0$ at non-critical level.

The open case is non-simply-laced and exceptional types,
where direct computation of the shadow tower for
non-principal orbits is not yet available.
\end{remark}

% ================================================================
% The shadow metric
% ================================================================

\subsection{The shadow metric}
\label{sec:shadow-metric}
\index{shadow metric|textbf}

\begin{definition}[Shadow metric]
\label{def:shadow-metric}
\index{shadow metric|textbf}
\index{critical discriminant|textbf}
A \emph{primary line}\index{primary line|textbf} in the
cyclic deformation complex $\Defcyc^{\mathrm{mod}}(\cA)$ is a
one-dimensional subspace $L = \Bbbk\cdot\eta$ spanned by a
single primary field~$\eta$ of definite conformal weight
$h_\eta$. For a chiral algebra with generators
$\phi_1,\dotsc,\phi_r$ of weights $h_1,\dotsc,h_r$,
each generator spans a primary line; the full deformation
space is the direct sum $\bigoplus_i L_i$ at first order.

Let $L \subset \Defcyc^{\mathrm{mod}}(\cA)$ be a
primary line with coordinate~$x$, shadow data
$\mathrm{Sh}_r|_L = S_r\,x^r$, curvature
$S_2 =: \kappa$, and cubic coefficient
$S_3 =: \alpha$. Define the \emph{weighted initial data}
$a_0 := 2\kappa$, $a_1 := 3\alpha$, $a_2 := 4S_4$.
The \emph{shadow quadratic form on~$L$} (or \emph{shadow metric},
by analogy with its role in controlling the tower geometry; not a
Riemannian metric) is the quadratic polynomial
\begin{equation}\label{eq:shadow-metric}
 Q_L(t)
 \;:=\;
 a_0^2
 \;+\;
 2\,a_0\,a_1\,t
 \;+\;
 \bigl(a_1^2 + 2\,a_0\,a_2\bigr)\,t^2
 \;=\;
 4\kappa^2 + 12\kappa\alpha\,t
 + \bigl(9\alpha^2 + 16\kappa S_4\bigr)\,t^2
 \;\in\; k(c)[t].
\end{equation}
The \emph{critical discriminant} of~$L$ is
\begin{equation}\label{eq:critical-discriminant}
 \Delta \;:=\; 8\,\kappa\,S_4
 \;=\; a_0\,a_2.
\end{equation}
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Riccati algebraicity: the shadow generating function
 is algebraic of degree~$2$]
\label{thm:riccati-algebraicity}
\label{thm:shadow-metric-existence}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{shadow generating function!algebraicity|textbf}
\index{Riccati algebraicity|textbf}
Let $L$, $S_r$, $Q_L$ be as in
Definition~\textup{\ref{def:shadow-metric}}.
Set $P = 1/\kappa$ and define the weighted shadow
generating function
$H(t) := \sum_{r \geq 2} r\,S_r\,t^r$.
The all-degree master equation
$\nabla_H(\mathrm{Sh}_r) + \mathfrak{o}^{(r)} = 0$
\textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:master-equation-from-mc})}
on~$L$ is equivalent to the algebraic relation
\begin{equation}\label{eq:riccati-algebraic-relation}
 H(t)^2
 \;=\;
 t^4\,Q_L(t),
\end{equation}
so that
\begin{equation}\label{eq:shadow-gf-closed-form}
 H(t)
 \;=\;
 t^2\sqrt{Q_L(t)}.
\end{equation}
The entire shadow obstruction tower on~$L$ is determined by three
genus-$0$ invariants $(\kappa, \alpha, S_4)$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
On~$L$ the master equation reduces to the recursion
\begin{equation}\label{eq:single-line-inversion}
 S_r
 \;=\;
 -\frac{P}{2r}
 \sum_{\substack{j + k = r + 2 \\ 3 \leq j \leq k}}
 c_{jk}\,jk\,S_j\,S_k,
 \qquad
 c_{jk} = \begin{cases} 1 & j < k, \\ 1/2 & j = k.\end{cases}
\end{equation}
The H-Poisson bracket
$\{S_j x^j,\,S_k x^k\}_H = jk\,P\,S_j S_k\,x^{j+k-2}$
and the eigenvalue identity
$\nabla_H(x^r) = 2r\,x^r$ separate the master equation into
the $\nabla_H$ term $2r\,S_r\,x^r$ on the left and the
obstruction sum on the right;
since $j \geq 3$ forces $k \leq r - 1$, the recursion is
well-defined.

Define $a_n := (n+2)\,S_{n+2}$ for $n \geq 0$, so that
$H(t) = \sum_{n \geq 0} a_n\,t^{n+2}$.
Then $a_0 = 2\kappa$, $a_1 = 3\alpha$,
$a_2 = 4S_4$. Setting $F(t) := H(t)/t^2
= \sum_{n \geq 0} a_n\,t^n$,
the claim~\eqref{eq:riccati-algebraic-relation} reads
$F(t)^2 = Q_L(t)$.

Expanding $F^2 = \sum_{m \geq 0}
\bigl(\sum_{i+j=m} a_i a_j\bigr)\,t^m$:
at $m = 0$, $a_0^2 = 4\kappa^2$;
at $m = 1$, $2a_0 a_1 = 12\kappa\alpha$;
at $m = 2$,
$a_1^2 + 2a_0 a_2 = 9\alpha^2 + 16\kappa S_4$.
These match the coefficients of~$Q_L(t)$
(Definition~\ref{def:shadow-metric}) at
orders~$0$, $1$, $2$ respectively.
For $m \geq 3$, the convolution identity
$\sum_{i+j=m} a_i a_j = 0$ is exactly the
recursion~\eqref{eq:single-line-inversion} at
degree $r = m + 2$: the MC equation forces all
higher Taylor coefficients of $F^2 - Q_L$ to vanish.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Riccati ODE reformulation]
\label{rem:riccati-ode}
\index{Riccati ODE|textbf}
\index{shadow generating function!Riccati ODE}
Set $U(t) := \sum_{r \geq 3} S_r\,t^r$ (the nonlinear part, removing
$\mathrm{Sh}_2$). The algebraic relation~\eqref{eq:riccati-algebraic-relation}
is equivalent to the Riccati-type first-order ODE
\begin{equation}\label{eq:riccati-ode-form}
 2t\,U'(t) \;+\; \frac{P}{2}\bigl(U'(t)\bigr)^2
 \;=\; R_3\,t^3 + R_4\,t^4,
\end{equation}
where $R_3 = 6\alpha$ and
$R_4 = (9\alpha^2 + 2\Delta)/(2\kappa)$ encode the cubic and quartic inputs.
The left-hand side is \emph{quadratic in~$U'$}: this is the precise sense in
which the shadow obstruction tower is algebraic of degree~$2$. All terms at $t^r$ for
$r \geq 5$ cancel identically, verified through
degree~$15$ by direct computation. The ODE~\eqref{eq:riccati-ode-form}
determines $U$ uniquely from $(\kappa, \alpha, S_4)$ by power-series inversion,
reproducing the MC recursion~\eqref{eq:single-line-inversion}.
\end{remark}

\begin{corollary}[Gaussian decomposition]
\label{cor:gaussian-decomposition}
\label{cor:shadow-algebraic-degree}
\index{shadow metric!Gaussian decomposition}
\index{shadow tower!algebraic degree}
\index{shadow depth!vs algebraic complexity}
The shadow metric decomposes as
\begin{equation}\label{eq:gaussian-decomposition}
 Q_L(t)
 \;=\;
 (2\kappa + 3\alpha\,t)^2
 \;+\;
 2\Delta\,t^2,
\end{equation}
where $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$ is the critical discriminant.
The first term $(2\kappa + 3\alpha\,t)^2 = (a_0 + a_1\,t)^2$
is the \emph{Gaussian envelope}: a perfect square, the shadow
metric of the truncated tower with $S_r = 0$ for $r \geq 4$.
The second term $2\Delta\,t^2 = 16\kappa S_4\,t^2$
is the \emph{interaction correction}.

Shadow depth $r_{\max} = \infty$ does not entail infinite
algebraic complexity.
Class~$\mathbf{M}$ has algebraic degree~$2$
over $k(c)[t]$: one rational parameter $\Delta$ controls
the entire infinite tower. The spectral curve
$\Sigma_L := \{H^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)\}$ in the
$(t,H)$-plane is rational (genus~$0$); the recursion operator
$S_r \mapsto S_{r+1}$ is the Higgs field whose
characteristic equation is~$\Sigma_L$.
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
$(2\kappa + 3\alpha t)^2 = 4\kappa^2 + 12\kappa\alpha\,t
+ 9\alpha^2 t^2$. Adding $2\Delta\,t^2 = 16\kappa S_4\,t^2$
gives $Q_L(t)$.
\end{proof}

The Gaussian decomposition reduces the entire shadow tower to a
single algebraic question: is $Q_L(t)$ a perfect square in
$k(c)[t]$? Because $Q_L$ is quadratic in~$t$, the answer is
governed by a classical dichotomy. A quadratic polynomial is a
perfect square if and only if its discriminant vanishes; when it
does, $\sqrt{Q_L}$ is linear and the tower terminates after
finitely many terms; when it does not, $\sqrt{Q_L}$ is
irrational over the polynomial ring and the binomial series for
$(1+u)^{1/2}$ produces infinitely many nonzero shadow
coefficients. There is no third possibility. The depth of the
shadow tower is not a free parameter: it is \emph{read off} from
the arithmetic of a single quadratic, and the only outcomes are
termination or infinite extent.

\begin{lemma}[Impossibility of $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 3$]
\label{lem:depth-three-impossible}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{algebraic depth!impossibility of three|textbf}
\index{depth gap!degree-4 MC obstruction}
Let $\cA$ be a chirally Koszul algebra on a primary line~$L$ with
$\kappa|_L \neq 0$. If $S_4 \neq 0$, then
$S_r \neq 0$ for infinitely many $r \geq 5$. In particular,
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 3$ is impossible on any $\kappa \neq 0$ line.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
Two independent arguments.

\emph{First proof: MC recursion propagation.}
Set $P = 1/\kappa$. The master
equation~\eqref{eq:single-line-inversion} at degree $r = 5$ gives
\[
 S_5
 \;=\;
 -\frac{P}{10}\cdot 3 \cdot 4\,S_3 S_4
 \;=\;
 -\frac{6}{5}\,P\,\alpha\,S_4,
\]
where $\alpha := S_3$.
If $\alpha \neq 0$ and $S_4 \neq 0$, then $S_5 \neq 0$
(since $P \neq 0$), so the tower extends past degree~$5$.
If $\alpha = 0$ (the only remaining case with $S_4 \neq 0$), then
$S_5 = 0$, but the recursion at $r = 6$ gives
\[
 S_6
 \;=\;
 -\frac{P}{12}\cdot\frac{1}{2}\cdot 4\cdot 4\,S_4^2
 \;=\;
 -\frac{2\,S_4^2}{3\kappa}
 \;\neq\; 0.
\]
In either case, $S_4 \neq 0$ forces a nonzero shadow past degree~$5$.
The closed form $H(t) = t^2\sqrt{Q_L(t)}$ with $Q_L = (2\kappa + 3\alpha\,t)^2 + 2\Delta\,t^2$
and $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4 \neq 0$ then gives the full conclusion:
$Q_L$ is not a perfect square, so $\sqrt{Q_L}$ has infinitely many
nonzero Taylor coefficients.

\emph{Second proof: shadow Lie algebra weight structure.}
On the primary line $L = \Bbbk \cdot x$, the Hamiltonian model of
the shadow algebra has bracket
$\{x^j, x^k\}_H = jk\,P\,x^{j+k-2}$ with $P = \kappa^{-1}$.
The shadow elements $h := \kappa x^2$, $\mathfrak{C} := S_3 x^3$,
$\mathfrak{Q} := S_4 x^4$ act by
\[
 D_h(x^r) = 2r\,x^r,\qquad
 D_{\mathfrak{C}}(x^r) = 3r\,P\,S_3\,x^{r+1},\qquad
 D_{\mathfrak{Q}}(x^r) = 4r\,P\,S_4\,x^{r+2}.
\]
The operator $D_h$ is a semisimple grading, while
$D_{\mathfrak{Q}}$ raises degree by~$2$.
If $S_4 \neq 0$ then
\[
 D_{\mathfrak{Q}}(x^4)
 \;=\;
 16\,P\,S_4\,x^6
 \;\neq\; 0,
\]
so the degree-$6$ weight space is populated.
Applying $D_{\mathfrak{Q}}$ again:
$D_{\mathfrak{Q}}(x^6) = 24\,P\,S_4\,x^8 \neq 0$; then
$D_{\mathfrak{Q}}(x^{2m}) = 4 \cdot 2m \cdot P\,S_4\,x^{2m+2} \neq 0$
for all $m \geq 2$.
By induction, $S_4 \neq 0$ forces nonzero shadows at
all even degrees $\geq 6$.
In particular, the tower cannot terminate at any finite degree
$\geq 5$: once the quartic shadow survives, infinitely many successors
are forced.

The two arguments identify the same structural constraint:
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} \in \{0, 1\}$ (finite tower, $S_4 = 0$) or
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = \infty$ ($S_4 \neq 0$, infinite tower) on any
$\kappa \neq 0$ primary line. The value $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 3$
falls in neither class.
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}[Algebraic depth gap: no $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 3$]
\label{prop:depth-gap-trichotomy}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
\index{algebraic depth!gap theorem|textbf}
\index{shadow depth!forbidden values}
\index{depth classification!algebraic depth gap}
Let $\cA$ be a chirally Koszul algebra in the standard landscape.
Then the algebraic depth satisfies
\begin{equation}\label{eq:depth-gap}
 d_{\mathrm{alg}}(\cA) \;\in\; \{0,\, 1,\, 2,\, \infty\}.
\end{equation}
More precisely:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item on any primary line~$L$ with $\kappa|_L \neq 0$, one has
 $d_{\mathrm{alg}}(L) \in \{0, 1, \infty\}$;
\item the boundary value $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 2$ is realized by the
 conformal-weight family $\beta\gamma_\lambda$
 \textup{(}equivalently $bc_\lambda$\textup{)}, whose global shadow
 data are
 \[
  S_2 = 6\lambda^2 - 6\lambda + 1,\qquad
  S_3 = 0,\qquad
  S_4 = -5/12,\qquad
  S_r = 0 \quad (r \geq 5);
 \]
\item no value $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 3$
 \textup{(}or any finite $d_{\mathrm{alg}} \geq 3$\textup{)}
 is realized.
\end{enumerate}
The four values correspond bijectively to the shadow depth
classes $\mathbf{G}$, $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, $\mathbf{M}$
of Definition~\textup{\ref{def:shadow-depth-classification}}.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
The argument has two parts: the single-line Riccati regime
$\kappa \neq 0$, and the global contact witness.

\emph{Single-line regime \textup{(}$\kappa \neq 0$\textup{)}.}
On a primary line~$L$ with $\kappa \neq 0$, the shadow generating
function $H(t) = t^2\sqrt{Q_L(t)}$ is determined by
$(\kappa, \alpha, S_4)$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:riccati-algebraicity}), where
$Q_L(t) = (2\kappa + 3\alpha\,t)^2 + 2\Delta\,t^2$
is the Gaussian decomposition
(Corollary~\ref{cor:gaussian-decomposition}) and
$\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$ is the critical discriminant
\eqref{eq:critical-discriminant}.

If $\Delta = 0$ (equivalently $S_4 = 0$), then
$Q_L(t) = (2\kappa + 3\alpha\,t)^2$ is a perfect square, so
$H(t) = t^2(2\kappa + 3\alpha\,t)$. This is a polynomial of
degree~$3$: the shadow coefficients $S_r = 0$ for all $r \geq 4$.
The tower terminates at degree at most~$3$, giving
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} \in \{0, 1\}$ according to whether
$\alpha = 0$
(class~$\mathbf{G}$, $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 0$),
or $\alpha \neq 0$ and $S_4 = 0$
(class~$\mathbf{L}$, $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 1$).

If $\Delta \neq 0$ (equivalently $S_4 \neq 0$), then $Q_L$ is not
a perfect square in $k(c)[t]$: the interaction correction
$2\Delta\,t^2$ prevents $\sqrt{Q_L}$ from being polynomial.
The Taylor expansion of $\sqrt{Q_L(t)}$ about $t = 0$ has
infinitely many nonzero coefficients (the binomial series for
$(1 + u)^{1/2}$ with $u = (6\alpha\,t + (9\alpha^2 +
2\Delta)\,t^2)/(4\kappa^2)$ does not terminate when the
coefficient of~$t^2$ in~$u$ is nonzero).
Therefore $S_r \neq 0$ for infinitely many~$r$, and
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = \infty$ (class~$\mathbf{M}$).

Thus no single primary line with $\kappa \neq 0$ can realize
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 2$ or~$3$.

The impossibility of $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 3$ (and any finite
$d_{\mathrm{alg}} \geq 3$) on a $\kappa \neq 0$ line is
Lemma~\ref{lem:depth-three-impossible}: if $S_4 \neq 0$,
the MC recursion at degrees $5$ and $6$ forces a nonzero tail
regardless of whether $\alpha = 0$ or $\alpha \neq 0$, and the
closed form $H(t) = t^2\sqrt{Q_L(t)}$ with $\Delta \neq 0$ makes
the infinite extent manifest.

\emph{Global contact witness.}
The value $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 2$ is realized by the
standard conformal-weight family $\beta\gamma_\lambda$, not by either of its
one-dimensional internal slices. On the weight-changing line,
all shadows vanish and $\mu_{\beta\gamma} = 0$ by rank-one abelian
rigidity
\textup{(}Corollary~\textup{\ref{cor:nms-betagamma-mu-vanishing}}\textup{)}.
On the $T$-line, Proposition~\ref{prop:betagamma-T-line-shadows}
